message.reasoning:
We need to produce a JSON response only, following the format. Need to decide kind: think|tool_call|complete. The user wants us to respond with JSON only, no markdown. The prior history shows we are at a point where we need to gather exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions. We have expanded file symbols for artifacts.rs and hydrated source. Now we need to locate schema version constants. Probably we need to search for symbols like FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION etc. The selected history shows we used graph.find and session.expand_file. Now we need to maybe read the content of the file to get exact constants. Use graph.resolve or maybe session.read? There's no read tool, but we can use session.expand_file? Already done. Maybe we need to hydrate_source? Already hydrated source, but not fully. We might need to get the actual code content to see the constants. There is a tool "session.hydrate_source" which hydrates source to view schema version definitions. We already called it but didn't get result? Actually tool_result_summary shows session.hydrate_source@v1 {status:ok,path:,line_count:0,match_count:0,fields:[changed,operation,session_export,status,target]}. No actual content. Maybe we need to use "graph.find" again with more specific regex to locate the constants. Or we could use "session.expand_file" with target selector to get the file content? The tool "session.expand_file" expands file symbols but doesn't show content. There is no "read" tool. Possibly we can use "graph.describe" to get description of a symbol, which might include its source? Or "session.hydrate" to get full source.

We can use "session.hydrate" perhaps? The tool contract includes session.hydrate@v1. That hydrates a target (maybe source). It might provide the source code. Could use "session.hydrate" with target selector pointing to the file or symbol. But we need to know the selector. Possibly use "symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION". Use session.hydrate with that symbol to get its definition.

We need to produce a tool call. So kind: tool_call, tool_name: session.hydrate@v1, payload with target selector. The selector likely is the symbol's logical_key? Eg "symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION". Use that.

We also need to include acquired_fact_ids: list of fact ids we are acquiring now? The format expects "acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"]; we can include the symbol id maybe "F1"? Actually fact ids are short ids like F1, S3 etc. The target is a symbol maybe with id? Could use "S10"? The schema version constant maybe has id S10 per graph relations: [F1] ... [S10] symbol:...::FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION. So we can acquire that fact id "S10". So acquired_fact_ids: ["S10"].

Readiness_state: after tool call, maybe "evidence_visible" or "ready_to_edit"? Since we are about to hydrate to get source, not yet evidence visible? Might be "not_ready". But after we get the source we may have evidence. But we are just making a tool call; readiness likely "not_ready" until we get result. The schema says readiness_state can be "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". At this point before tool call, we are not ready? Actually we have some evidence but not exact. So we could set "evidence_visible"? The instructions: "When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names". So we need to output JSON with kind:"tool_call", assistant_message:"<brief status>", tool_call:{...}, acquired_fact_ids:["<fact id>"], readiness_state:"..."}.

Assistant_message should be brief status, maybe "hydrating schema version definition". Provide that.

Thus output:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Hydrating FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION symbol",
  "tool_call":{
    "tool_name":"session.hydrate@v1",
    "payload":{
      "target":"symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION"
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids":["S10"],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

Make sure no extra fields. Ensure JSON only. No trailing commas. Use double quotes exactly.

Check that we included correct payload fields: session.hydrate takes target selector and optional padding and hydration budgets. We can just include target. That's enough.

Thus final answer.
